S-PRIVACY-IR.notes: privacy_ir.rs: canonical typed set-intersection, private-lookup, and aggregate operations admit PSI/PIR/VDAF/MPC/TEE/FHE/ZK lowerings only when backend-specific named trust roots, semantic leakage and composition budgets, workload key holders/epochs, fail-closed recovery, and non-guarantees are explicit. A separate reference interpreter and lowered interpreter agree for every admitted backend; UI summaries preserve trust/non-guarantee names. Falsifiers reject unsupported downgrade, hidden TEE/committee trust, underdeclared/composed leakage, permanent global keys, duplicate roots, and operation/shape/backend ambiguity. This implements the local IR/admission claim, not any backend cryptosystem's security.

M-HFHE-SUITE.notes: hfhe.rs + refresh.rs: exact deterministic F_(2^127-1) arithmetic and shape-bound ciphertext-as-circuit encoding are implemented as a conformance/cryptanalysis precursor, while the prior Z/(2^64) LWE-shaped ciphertext is explicitly a negative control. The boundary always reports registered=false/enabled=false and local activation rejects. Wrong-field downgrade, gate splice, noncanonical field, ciphertext shape ambiguity, and unsupported suite dispatch reject. The row stays PARTIAL: no IND model, standard-assumption reduction, independent concrete attack estimate, production parameters, complete public refresh prover, two independent verifier families, or rotation evidence exists.

M-PROOF-CARRYING-REFRESH.notes: refresh.rs: dispatch binds proof suite, source/destination suites, both epochs, source/destination rights and policy roots, chain, job, transcript, fresh challenge window, both shape-bound ciphertext commitments, and plaintext continuity digest. Stateful verification rejects missing/stale/replayed challenges, epoch rollback, suite/policy/rights/context mutation, ciphertext substitution, proof tamper and cross-transcript splice; retired secret/audit keys expose an explicit zeroized state and reject use. The row stays PARTIAL because continuity is a symmetric audit-key tag over a toy negative-control ciphertext, not a complete ZK prover, and only one local verifier family exists.

N-TEE-FIBER.notes: tee_fiber.rs integrates against noos-nel PromptJob/PrivacyProfile/FinalityClass public interfaces and defines a hardware-quote verifier boundary binding encrypted prompt commitment, exact job/model/decoding/numeric profile, executor identity/binary, firmware/vendor trust, policy, output, fresh challenge, expiry, and monotonic rollback counter. Receipts are exactly ASSURED_TEE, name vendor trust, reject every NEL open/assured/proven settlement class, and private-profile rollback preserves historical receipts while disabling new jobs. Tests cover every field mutation, stale/replayed/rollback attestations, firmware downgrade, cross-job prompt splice, leakage observations, and prompt/key zeroization state. The row stays PARTIAL because the verifier fixture is not real vendor hardware and no preregistered real-hardware timing/length campaign or confidential arbitration evidence exists.

E-NEL-08.notes: tee_fiber.rs provides executable local falsifiers for complete attestation mutation, relay across job/model/profile, challenge replay, stale expiry, monotonic-counter rollback, firmware downgrade, cross-job prompt separation, equal-public-behavior length/duration-bucket budget accounting, and attempts to spend TEE receipts as NEL Soft/Anchored/Assured/Proven. All reject or remain inside the zero-delta local fixture budget, and rollback disables only the private profile. The row stays PARTIAL: no real TEE, vendor, prompt side-channel timing campaign, public-duration evidence, or declared confidential-arbitration threshold/adversary has been exercised.

E-NEL-PRIVATE-01.notes: lib.rs + transformer.rs: deterministic small-transformer BESI precursor physically pads every remote public-weight GEMM to 128 rows, split-shares over Z/(2^64), reconstructs raw accumulators, derives two post-response transcript-bound fresh exact-Z Freivalds challenges, and slices/requantizes only after acceptance; nonlinear/attention/KV-equivalent/residual work remains local. Tests cover all six GEMM tamper positions, challenge counts, executor-visible rows, channel replay/context/direction/epoch binding, canonical matrix shape ambiguity, raw-share DA rejection, adjudication tamper/rebind/replay/unknown-suite, exact ASSURED_SPLIT separation, and sanitized field names. The row stays PARTIAL: the deterministic fixture is not OS-CSPRNG sharing or independent executors, and it does not regenerate the frozen real Qwen2.5-0.5B 8/8-GEMM, 25-test, timing, or transport evidence.
